selection_note: Only candidate that is fresh, in-window, primary-sourced, and survives honest fact-checking. Dryad wildfire collapsed to company-PR sourcing; MONTIS is remote-piloted (not AI). Deck thesis: AI compute's gigawatt appetite is pressing on a Cook Inlet gas system that cannot even secure winter storage, and on July 10 the regulator rejected the fix.

weak_spots.handling: These three are sourced ONLY to the Lesil McGuire ADN op-ed (2026-07-06). On slides, attribute explicitly ('per a former state senator's op-ed' / 'op-ed') or use as framing context, never as a flat unhedged fact. Prefer the rock-solid RCA/ADN facts as the deck's factual spine.
weak_spots.derived_numbers_banned: Do NOT assert a derived urban-Alaska-peak-MW number. The verified comparison is Northern Journal's exact wording: 'a gigawatt or more, some 30% more than urban Alaska's peak demand' (c13). State it that way; do not compute an MW figure.
weak_spots.stak_money: STAK is 'more than $10 billion' at full buildout (c14); an earlier $500M reference exists in the same article. If used, say $10B+ and do not conflate.

claims.statement: The Regulatory Commission of Alaska rejected Enstar's request for an advance determination that its gas-storage project plans were prudent, citing missing information (an 'extraordinary request' that 'lacked basic information about the project').
claims.value: RCA denied Enstar's advance determination of prudency

claims.statement: The RCA order denying the plan was 17 pages long.
Enstar's proposed Cook Inlet gas storage facility would cost $240 million.
The project would add up to $12 a month to the average customer's bill.
Enstar proposed buying the Kenai Loop Pool, a depleted gas reservoir, from AIX Energy for use as storage.
claims.value: Kenai Loop Pool depleted reservoir, purchased from AIX Energy

claims.statement: The facility would initially hold 25 billion cubic feet of gas, close to half the utility's annual demand.
claims.value: 25 billion cubic feet, close to half annual demand

claims.statement: The Alaska DNR has not yet completed its determination that the Kenai Loop Pool is technically capable of serving as a natural gas storage facility.
Enstar and Hilcorp Cook Inlet have competing applications with the Alaska DNR for gas storage facility leases at the Kenai Loop Pool.
claims.value: Enstar vs Hilcorp competing DNR lease applications

claims.statement: Without the commission's approval, Enstar president John Sims said the company has suspended its plans to move ahead with the project.
Statewide, Alaska residential electricity rates run roughly 40% higher than the national average (former state senator's op-ed).
claims.attribution_required: per a former state senator's op-ed

claims.statement: In many rural Alaska communities, energy costs are more than twice those in more urban parts of Alaska (op-ed).
Southcentral Alaska faces a projected Cook Inlet natural gas shortfall by 2027 (op-ed).
STAK Energy's proposed North Slope AI data-center campus generators could produce a gigawatt or more of power, some 30% more than urban Alaska's peak demand.
claims.value: a gigawatt or more; some 30% more than urban Alaska's peak demand

claims.statement: STAK Energy's North Slope project would cost more than $10 billion at full buildout (an earlier $500 million reference appears in the same article).
More than 500 public comments were received on the STAK North Slope lease before the preliminary deadline; fewer than a dozen endorsed the project.
claims.value: 500+ comments; fewer than a dozen in favor

claims.statement: The STAK ADL 422741 public comment window was extended to 4:30 p.m. July 17, 2026, citing comment volume and public interest.

unused_but_verified.note: Alt-cluster claims verified but NOT selected (kept for record).
unused_but_verified.dryad_xprize: XPRIZE Wildfire is a $11M four-year competition; teams autonomously detect+suppress a fire in a 1,000 km2 area within 10 minutes; finals held in rural Alaska June 15 to 25, 2026 (xprize.org). Dryad performance claims are company-PR-only.
unused_but_verified.montis: FAA approved MONTIS avalanche-control drone (DroneLife 2026-07-09); it is REMOTELY PILOTED, not AI/autonomous; works alongside helicopters (not replacing); Alaska DOT is a collaboration partner; Seward Highway passes under 200 avalanche paths; 49 road hits Oct-Mar; $1.1M SMART grant.
